sourceMovie.title: Fireworks
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Fireworks (2017) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Arom Choi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gabriel's Inferno: Part III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fireworks clicked for you, Gabriel's Inferno: Part III (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fireworks?
faq.answer: Try Gabriel's Inferno: Part III, The Secret: Dare to Dream, Newness—each shares DNA with Fireworks while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fireworks?
faq.answer: Fireworks (2017) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fireworks worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
